rationale.R1.2: A review of this section centrally includes checking whether the stability, convergence, and performance claims cohere with one another.
rationale.R1.3: The section makes explicit claims about benchmarks, speedups, order, and stability that should be checked against the reported evidence within the artifact.
rationale.R1.6: A generic section review normally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, and register.
rationale.RX.1: The reviewer should disclose uncertainty because the artifact provides claims without full proofs, tables, figures, or methodological details.
rationale.RX.2: Overclaim avoidance is load-bearing because the section asserts unconditional A-stability while also describing a conditional stability regime.
rationale.RX.3: The task is only to review the section, so staying within that artifact and delegation is essential.
rationale.RX.4: Downstream-harm avoidance is active because overstated numerical stability or performance claims could mislead later technical use.
rationale.RX.5: Traceability is needed so any projected review responsibility remains tied to specific claims in the provided section.
